response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and systems experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with some exposure to Java and C/C++ on a CVI IDE at National Instruments over a decade ago. However, the role demands deep, current expertise in Java, Kotlin, Spring, and JVM internals, none of which appear as recent or substantial experience. Kotlin is absent, Spring/Hibernate/Quarkus are not mentioned, and there is no evidence of recent IDE/language-tooling work on the JVM. The profile fits a polyglot product engineer, not a JVM tooling specialist.
strengths: Past IDE/tooling exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++)
Strong testing/TDD culture and developer-tooling mindset (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, workshops)
Proven ability to work across complex systems and large codebases over a long career
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minimal, dated Java exposure
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM framework experience
No recent JVM internals or modern IDE-platform (e.g., IntelliJ Platform) work

key_factors.factor: Lack of Kotlin and modern Java/Spring expertise
No recent JVM or IDE-platform tooling work
Prior C/C++ IDE component experience at National Instruments
